content.problemStatement:
A city has been infected by a contagious virus.

In a survey, it was found that AAA out of the NNN people living in the city are currently infected.
It has been observed that the only way for a person to get infected is if he comes in contact with an already infected person, and both of them are NOT wearing a mask.

The mayor of the city wants to make a new Mask Policy and find out the minimum number of people that will be required to wear a mask to avoid the further spread of the virus.
Help the mayor in finding this number.

Note: The only aim of the mayor is to stop virus spread, not to mask every infected person.


Input Format

The first line contains TTT - number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains two integers NNN and AAA - the total number of people living in the city and the number of people already affected by the virus respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum number of people that will be required to wear a mask so as to curb the virus spread.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers N and A — the total population of the city and the number of already infected people respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer on its own line — the minimum number of people that must wear a mask so that no further infection can occur.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: N=2, A=1 → min(1,1)=1.\nTest case 2: N=3, A=2 → min(2,1)=1.\nTest case 3: N=3, A=1 → min(1,2)=1.
N=5, A=2 → min(2,3)=2. Mask the two infected people.
N=10, A=9 → min(9,1)=1. Mask the single uninfected person.

solution.approach: Infection can only happen when an infected person meets an uninfected person and both are unmasked. To stop any future infection we must guarantee that at least one member of every possible infected‑uninfected pair wears a mask. The cheapest way is to mask the smaller group: either all infected people (A) or all uninfected people (N‑A). Therefore the answer for each test case is min(A, N‑A). The algorithm is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it)); A = int(next(it))
        out.append(str(min(A, N - A)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
